1→"""Codex CLI prompt execution implementation. 2→ 3→This module provides the CodexPromptExecutor implementation that maps erk's 4→PromptExecutor ABC to OpenAI's Codex CLI. Codex uses a completely different 5→CLI interface and JSONL streaming format from Claude. 6→ 7→See docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-cli-reference.md for flag mapping. 8→See docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md for output format. 9→""" 10→ 11→from __future__ import annotations 12→ 13→import logging 14→import os 15→import shutil 16→import subprocess 17→import sys 18→import tempfile 19→import threading 20→from collections.abc import Iterator 21→from pathlib import Path 22→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 23→ 24→from erk.core.codex_output_parser import CodexParserState, parse_codex_jsonl_line 25→from erk_shared.context.types import permission_mode_to_codex_exec, permission_mode_to_codex_tui 26→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import ( 27→ ErrorEvent, 28→ ExecutorEvent, 29→ NoOutputEvent, 30→ NoTurnsEvent,

session.tool_result: Found 9 files src/erk/core/context.py tests/unit/core/test_codex_prompt_executor.py tests/unit/core/test_context.py src/erk/core/codex_prompt_executor.py docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md docs/learned/integrations/index.md docs/learned/integrations/tripwires.md docs/learned/integrations/codex-integration.md docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-cli-reference.md
session.assistant: Let me check the documentation files to see how much needs updating there:
session.tool_result:
docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md:5: - "implementing a CodexPromptExecutor" docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md:16: warning: "The two formats share almost nothing structurally. A CodexPromptExecutor needs its own parser — don't parameterize the existing Claude parser." docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md:34:The natural instinct is to parameterize the existing Claude parser for Codex. This won't work — the formats share almost nothing structurally. Claude uses a single-level `type` field (`assistant`/`user`/`result`) with content nested in `message.content[]` blocks. Codex uses a completely different two-level type system with flattened item fields. A `CodexPromptExecutor` needs its own `_parse_jsonl_line()` method built from the format spec below. docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md:143:These differences explain why a `CodexPromptExecutor` needs entirely separate parsing logic: docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md:157:Claude's `type: "result"` event includes `num_turns`, which erk uses to detect hook blocking (emitted as `NoTurnsEvent`). Codex has no equivalent field. A future `CodexPromptExecutor` would need a different strategy for hook-blocking detection — for example, checking whether any `item.*` events appeared between `turn.started` and `turn.completed`. Without this, `NoTurnsEvent` and `NoOutputEvent` diagnostics cannot be ported directly. docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-cli-reference.md:8: - "building a CodexPromptExecutor or Codex-aware AgentLauncher" docs/learned/integrations/codex-integration.md:17:## CodexPromptExecutor docs/learned/integrations/index.md:9:- **[codex/codex-cli-reference.md](codex/codex-cli-reference.md)** — implementing Codex backend support in erk, mapping PermissionMode to Codex sandbox flags, building a CodexPromptExecutor or Codex-aware AgentLauncher, understanding Claude CLI features that have no Codex equivalent docs/learned/integrations/index.md:10:- **[codex/codex-jsonl-format.md](codex/codex-jsonl-format.md)** — parsing codex exec --json output, implementing a CodexPromptExecutor, mapping Codex events to erk ExecutorEvent types, comparing Claude stream-json and Codex JSONL formats docs/learned/integrations/tripwires.md:41:**reusing ClaudePromptExecutor parsing logic for Codex** → Read [Codex CLI JSONL Output Format](codex/codex-jsonl-format.md) first. The two formats share almost nothing structurally. A CodexPromptExecutor needs its own parser — don't parameterize the existing Claude parser.
[Showing results with pagination = limit: 20, offset: 0]

session.tool_result:
commit 3d373c75d04e2ca4dedc6794de18e922ebc788b1 Author: Nick Schrock <schrockn@dagsterlabs.com> Date: Fri Mar 6 15:18:39 2026 -0800
Add plan: Plan: Flat prompt executor namespace + kill LlmCaller
.erk/impl-context/plan.md | 225 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ .erk/impl-context/ref.json | 4 + 2 files changed, 229 insertions(+)
session.assistant: Let me read that plan:
session.tool_result:
1→# Plan: Flat prompt executor namespace + kill LlmCaller 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→The codebase has two parallel abstractions for LLM calls: `PromptExecutor` (subprocess-based, full-featured) and `LlmCaller` (SDK-based, narrow). `LlmCaller` is a thin adapter that today delegates its CLI fallback to `PromptExecutor.execute_prompt()` anyway. Killing `LlmCaller` simplifies the model: everything is a `PromptExecutor`, the Anthropic SDK path becomes `AnthropicApiPromptExecutor`, and a composite `FallbackPromptExecutor` handles the API-first/CLI-fallback routing. 6→ 7→Simultaneously rename CLI-specific executors to be explicit about their nature. 8→ 9→## End state 10→ 11→``` 12→src/erk/core/ 13→ prompt_executor.py → ClaudeCliPromptExecutor (renamed) 14→ codex_prompt_executor.py → CodexCliPromptExecutor (renamed) 15→ anthropic_prompt_executor.py → AnthropicApiPromptExecutor (new) 16→ fallback_prompt_executor.py → FallbackPromptExecutor (new composite) 17→ fast_llm.py → deleted 18→ 19→packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/ 20→ llm_caller.py → deleted 21→ fakes.py → FakeLlmCaller, LlmCall removed 22→``` 23→ 24→`ErkContext.llm_caller` removed. All callers use `ctx.prompt_executor.execute_prompt()`. 25→ 26→--- 27→ 28→## Step 1: Rename `ClaudePromptExecutor` → `ClaudeCliPromptExecutor` 29→ 30→**`src/erk/core/prompt_executor.py`** — rename the class only.
... [201 lines omitted] ...
55→ - Maps `TextBlock` → `PromptResult(success=True, output=text, error=None)` 56→ - Maps `APIError` → `PromptResult(success=False, output="", error=str(exc))` 58→- `execute_command_streaming(...)` → `raise NotImplementedError` 59→- `execute_interactive(...)` → `raise NotImplementedError` 60→- `execute_prompt_passthrough(...)` → `raise NotImplementedError` 160→- Replace `isinstance(result, (NoApiKey, LlmCallFailed))` with `not result.success` 175→- Replace `isinstance(result, (NoApiKey, LlmCallFailed))` with `not result.success` 181→- Collapse `isinstance(result, NoApiKey)` / `isinstance(result, LlmCallFailed)` → `not result.success`

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on a snippet of the edited file: 1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→""" 3→Rename CodexPromptExecutor to CodexCliPromptExecutor across the codebase. 4→ 5→ONLY renames CodexPromptExecutor - does NOT touch ClaudePromptExecutor. 6→Only renames the class definition and references - does NOT rename the file itself. 7→ 8→Example: 9→ python refactor_codex_executor.py src/erk/core/codex_prompt_executor.py src/erk/core/context.py tests/unit/core/test_codex_prompt_executor.py tests/unit/core/test_context.py --dry-run 10→ python refactor_codex_executor.py src/erk/core/codex_prompt_executor.py src/erk/core/context.py tests/unit/core/test_codex_prompt_executor.py tests/unit/core/test_context.py 11→""" 12→ 13→import sys 14→from pathlib import Path 15→ 16→import libcst as cst 17→from libcst import matchers as m 18→ 19→ 20→class RenameCodexExecutorTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer): 21→ """Rename ONLY CodexPromptExecutor to CodexCliPromptExecutor. 22→ 23→ Does NOT touch ClaudePromptExecutor. 24→ """ 25→ 26→ def __init__(self): 27→ super().__init__() 28→ self.changes_made = 0 29→

""" if global_config is not None and global_config.interactive_agent.backend == "codex": - return CodexPromptExecutor(console=console) + return CodexCliPromptExecutor(console=console) return ClaudePromptExecutor(console=console)
